mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or rolling Rank IC remains below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth exceeds 1% quarterly but 20-day forward returns remain negative in > 60% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation months, or Rank IC > 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined positive payroll growth and negative unemployment change yield negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth exceeds trend but 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of quarters, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or rolling Rank IC remains above 0.02.
